description: How to file complaints, seek redressal, and escalate issues with government and private entities

cpgrams.purpose: Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System — complaints against central government

rti.purpose: File RTI applications to central government ministries

lokpal.purpose: Complaints against public servants for corruption

ncpcr.purpose: National Commission for Protection of Child Rights

rti.what: Right to Information — get information from any public authority
how: File application at rtionline.gov.in (central) or state RTI portal
If denied or no response → First Appeal to senior officer within 30 days
If still unsatisfied → Second Appeal to Information Commission within 90 days
rti.tips: Be specific in your question. Ask for documents/records, not opinions. One subject per application.

consumer_forum.what: Three-tier quasi-judicial body for consumer disputes
how: No lawyer required (but recommended for complex cases)
Attach proof: bills, receipts, correspondence, photos
consumer_forum.fee: Rs. 200 to Rs. 5,000 depending on claim amount
